key_themes: Strong organic SaaS/ARR growth and customer retention
Microsoft WorkiQ trademark sale for $10m cash injection
Bullish analyst consensus (Strong Buy, ~55% average upside)
AI product innovation (ControliQ Series 5, CaseWorkiQ) with improving profitability
sources_checked: ActiveOps investor relations (H1 FY26 results, FY25 report, presentations)
LSE RNS announcements (Microsoft deal, trading updates)
Analyst consensus (Canaccord, multiple brokers via MarketBeat/Investing.com)
X posts on results and Microsoft deal
summary: ActiveOps reported strong H1 FY26 results with organic SaaS revenue up ~22% (constant currency), ARR reaching £40.6m (organic growth 27% CCY), NRR of 114%, and Adj. EBITDA doubling to £2.0m, building on double-digit FY25 growth. The April 2026 sale of WorkiQ trademarks to Microsoft for $10m provides a material cash boost (company remains debt-free with ~£13m cash previously) with no disruption to core products or roadmap. Analysts maintain Strong Buy ratings with average targets ~317p (vs. recent ~202-205p share price), citing growth momentum. Biggest risk is execution on sales scaling, acquisition integration (Enlighten), and competition in the AI decision intelligence space amid ongoing R&D/GTM investments.

recent_posts.highlight: Announced sale of WorkiQ trademarks to Microsoft for $10m; board to outline capital use with July FY results.
Stock +3% on Microsoft trademark deal news; capital deployment details expected in July.
Post-results interview highlighting 55% ARR growth (organic 24-27%), strong revenue momentum.
